name: Farmers And Merchants Savings Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Iowa City and Johnson County since 1901, offering mortgages, commercial loans, agricultural financing, and online banking services.
description_long:
Farmers & Merchants Savings Bank is a locally-rooted community bank that has served Johnson County, Iowa since 1901. With 125 years of operational history, the institution positions itself as a relationship-focused lender deeply integrated into its regional agricultural and business community. The bank maintains physical locations in Iowa City, Lone Tree, and Nichols, and prominently features endorsement from Coach Dan Gable, suggesting strong local brand recognition and community ties.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of lending products across three primary segments: agricultural loans (livestock loans, ag lines of credit, machinery financing), commercial/business loans (working capital lines, construction loans, equipment financing), and residential real estate (mortgages including FHA and VA loans, construction loans, in-house and secondary market servicing). Consumer services include auto loans, personal loans, and internet/mobile banking with account access, balance checking, and online transfers. Their current advertised special is a 3.75% APY 125th Anniversary CD. All deposits are FDIC-insured.

The bank's primary distinguishing factor is its explicit focus on agricultural lending and rural/small-town community finance, positioning itself as understanding the specific financing needs of farmers and small business owners. Their in-house loan servicing for mortgages and emphasis on local decision-making differentiate them from larger regional or national banks. The institution markets itself around relationship banking and customized solutions rather than commoditized products.

The honest assessment is that this is a solid regional community bank with legitimate FDIC protections and competitive rates, but with limited geographic footprint (three locations in one Iowa county) and no indication of specialized services, rewards programs, or technological sophistication beyond basic online banking. For customers outside their service area or those seeking advanced fintech features, this bank is not accessible. The website contains no information on fees, minimum balances, rate competitiveness benchmarks, or customer service metrics. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Ag lines of credit (working capital and seasonal support)
Commercial loans and business lines of credit
Internet banking with balance checking, transfers, and account history
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
125 years of continuous operation since 1901, demonstrating institutional stability
Specialized agricultural lending programs tailored to local farmers and ag businesses
In-house mortgage servicing provides local decision-making and customer relationships
Offers FHA and VA loan programs for first-time home buyers and veterans
Multiple lending products under one institution (ag, commercial, consumer, real estate)
Mobile app and internet banking available for remote account access
cons: Geographic footprint limited to three small Iowa towns (Iowa City, Lone Tree, Nichols) — not accessible to most U.S. consumers
No information provided on interest rates, fees, minimum balances, or product competitiveness
No mention of savings accounts, checking account features, or deposit account types beyond CDs
Website provides minimal detail on application processes, approval timelines, or lending requirements
No information about customer service availability, phone support hours, or complaint resolution processes

contact.address: 2235 Mormon Trek Blvd, Iowa City, IA 52246

best_for: Iowa farmers and agricultural operators seeking specialized farm financing and equipment loans
Small business owners and commercial enterprises in Johnson County requiring local lending relationships
First-time home buyers and rural residents in their service area seeking mortgage and construction financing
Existing customers in Iowa City, Lone Tree, and Nichols seeking convenient local banking with online access
diagnosis: Farmers & Merchants Savings Bank is best for agricultural producers, small business owners, and home buyers within Johnson County, Iowa who value local relationship banking and face-to-face service. The primary caveat is extreme geographic limitation—the bank operates only three branches in rural Iowa, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this region and unsuitable for those seeking modern fintech features or rate/fee transparency.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 2235 Mormon Trek Blvd, Iowa City, IA 52246
